DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF MILES ROBERTSON'S MOTION TO COMPEL THE DEPOSITION OF DEFENDANT THE BAY CLUBS COMPANY, LLCS PERSON MOST KNOWLEDGEABLE; REQUEST FOR ORDER AWARDING MONETARY SANCTIONS. IF THE DEPOSITION TOOK PLACE AS OFFERED ON JANUARY 13, 2023, THE MOTION TO COMPEL IS DENIED AS MOOT. IF THE DEPOSITION HAS NOT OCCURRED, THE MOTION TO COMPEL IS GRANTED AND THE DEPOSITION MUST TAKE PLACE NO LATER THAN JANUARY 31, 2023, UNLESS OTHERWISE AGREED BY THE PARTIES. BOTH REQUESTS FOR SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. THE MEET AND CONFER CORRESPONDENCE INDICATES THE PARTIES WERE DISCUSSING THE SCHEDULING, WHICH WEIGHS IN FAVOR OF DEFENDANT'S REQUEST, BUT DEFENDANT DID NOT SERVE A FORMAL OBJECTION, WHICH WEIGHS IN FAVOR OF FINDING THE MOTION PERMISSIBLE. ON BALANCE, I CONCLUDE BOTH SIDES ACTED WITH SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER DIRECTLY TO THE JUDGE PRO TEM AT SBORROWMAN@GMAIL.COM. JUDGE PRO TEM: SCOTT BORROWMAN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

Off Calendar Mar-13-2023 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. JOSEPH L. CAGIGAS, JR. of Albert G. Stoll, Jr. / A Law Corporation (Tel: 415-576-1500) for plaintiff;
Atty. JENNIFER CAPABIANCO, Esq. of Hawkins Parnell & Young LLP (Tel: 415-766-3200) for defendant the Bay Clubs Company, LLC.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF MILES ROBERTSON's Motion To Compel Defendant The Bay Clubs Company LLC's Production Of Documents Nos. 2 And 3 And Demand For Sanctions.

Both sides stipulate to the matter being decided by the pro tem judge. The pro tem judge and counsel for both sides (all appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court takes the matter under submission.

Judge Pro Tem: Robert Kane; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Alternatively Summary Adjudication Of Issues
Off Calendar Feb-24-2023 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT THE BAY CLUBS COMPANY, LLC MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Alternatively Summary Adjudication Of Issues

Appearances (via ZOOM videoconference):
Jennifer Capabianco, Esq. of HAWKINS PARNELL & YOUNG, LLP, Tel: 415.979.2012, for defenant/moving party The Bay Clubs Company, LLC;
Joseph Cagigas, Esq. of ALBERT C. STOLL, JR., A LAW CORPORATION, Tel: 415.576.1500, for plaintiff/opposing party.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Defendant The Bay Clubs Company, Llcs Person Most Knowledgeable; Request For Order Awarding Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jan-18-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF MILES ROBERTSON's Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Defendant The Bay Clubs Company, Llcs Person Most Knowledgeable; Request For Order Awarding Monetary Sanctions.

If the deposition took place as offered on January 13, 2023, the motion to compel is DENIED as moot. If the deposition has not occurred, the motion to compel is GRANTED and the deposition must take place no later than January 31, 2023, unless otherwise agreed by the parties. Both requests for sanctions are DENIED. The meet and confer correspondence indicates the parties were discussing the scheduling, which weighs in favor of Defendant's request, but Defendant did not serve a formal objection, which weighs in favor of finding the motion permissible. On balance, I conclude both sides acted with substantial justification.

Prevailing party to prepare a form of order directly to the judge pro tem at sborrowman@gmail.com

Judge Pro Tem: Scott Borrowman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
